title: Boat Rental in Stockholm: Costs, Tips and Best Boats for 2026
html:
Planning a boating trip from Stockholm can be surprisingly affordable, with skippered day charters starting around €80 per person and self-drive motorboats from €125 for a half-day. Whether you plan to rent a motor yacht or a sailing catamaran, BoatHire24 connects you with verified owners across the archipelago. The best departure points include the marinas at Nybroviken and Strandvägen, where local operators are based.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Stockholm?

Prices for boat hire in Stockholm start at around €125 for a half-day motorboat and can exceed €2,500 for a luxury crewed yacht. A shared tour on a restored 1920s sailing boat typically costs €80 per person for a three-hour outing. A modern 25-foot motorboat, suitable for a family of five, is available from €250 for a full day, often including basic safety gear.

For larger groups, a Lagoon 42 catamaran can be chartered from €900 per day without skipper, or around €1,300 with a professional captain. Luxury motor yachts like the Princess F70 for hire come fully crewed and offer onboard amenities such as a galley, cabins and a flybridge. These can be booked for day trips or overnight stays, with prices starting at €2,200 per day depending on the season.

Where to set off in Stockholm

Most boat rentals in Stockholm depart from the city's central waterfront, with the main marinas being Nybroviken, Strandvägen and Djurgården. From Nybroviken you can reach the inner archipelago in under 30 minutes. The island of Fjäderholmarna, just 2 nautical miles east, is a popular first stop for lunch.

Other departure points include the Gärdet marina near the Värtahamnen ferry terminal and the Nacka Strand marina south of the city. From these you can explore islands such as Vaxholm, Grinda and Sandhamn, all within a 90-minute cruise. The waters are generally calm, with the exception of the designated shipping lanes.

For those wishing to explore further, a full-day trip from Nybroviken to the outer archipelago covers about 30 nautical miles round trip, passing through the narrow straits near Dalarö. Many charters provide suggested itineraries, and you can find a skippered charter that matches your group size.

Which boat to rent

  1. Motorboat 20-25 ft. Best for a family of four to explore nearby islands; half-day from €125.
  2. Skippered sailing yacht 35-45 ft. Ideal for small groups wanting a sailing experience; day charter from €400.
  3. Catamaran 38-45 ft. Spacious option for two couples or a family of six; full day from €900.
  4. Luxury motor yacht 55-80 ft. Crewed with cabins and flybridge, for up to 12 guests; from €2,200 per day.

How much does a boat rental cost in Stockholm?

  • Shared boat tour (3 hours, up to 12 guests): from €80 per person.
  • Small motorboat half-day (4 hours, up to 5 guests): from €125.
  • Modern 25-ft motorboat full day (8 hours, up to 6 guests): from €250.
  • Catamaran full day (up to 10 guests, skipper optional): from €900.
  • Crewed luxury yacht full day (up to 12 guests, inclusive of crew and fuel): from €2,200.

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Stockholm?

No, Sweden does not require a boating licence for pleasure craft under 10 metres length or with an engine power below 25 hp. However, many rental operators require a brief safety briefing and may impose age limits. For larger or more powerful boats, such as the Princess F70, a professional skipper is always included. The exact requirements should be confirmed with the operator before booking. The booking FAQ also covers typical licence policies for hire in Sweden.

In July the southwesterly breeze strengthens around midday so plan morning passages for a calm crossing to Fjäderholmarna.

At a glance

  • Price band: €80 (shared tour) to €2,500 (crewed yacht) per day.
  • Peak months: June, July and August.
  • Trip length: 3 hours (shared) to 8+ hours (full-day charter).
  • Guest capacity: 1 to 12 people typically.
  • Currency: EUR.

Questions about boat rental in Stockholm

Can I rent a boat in Stockholm without a licence?

Yes, many smaller boats can be hired without a licence, though safety briefings are standard.

What is the best month to rent a boat in Stockholm?

July offers the warmest weather and calmest seas, but June and August also provide good conditions with fewer crowds.

How far can I travel in a day from Stockholm?

A typical full-day trip covers 20 to 30 nautical miles, easily reaching Sandhamn or Grinda and back.

Are there overnight charter options?

Yes, many luxury yachts and catamarans offer multi-day charters with onboard cabins and galley.

Whether you are a first-time visitor or a seasoned sailor, boat rental in Stockholm opens up the archipelago’s 30,000 islands. BoatHire24 is a worldwide marketplace where you can rent a boat in Stockholm or list your own. To plan your trip, check availability directly or browse local owner listings for your ideal vessel.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Stockholm and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
